human_verification.notes: Reviewed issue #1063 requirements, physician-compensation operations boundary discipline, CMS physician billing and RVU sources, incident-to and attribution controls, Stark-law and OIG compliance boundaries, scenario coverage, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

review_notes: All required scenario families for the issue and cross-industry research contract are covered and mapped to passing results.
All required functional abilities for execution-processing-and-fulfillment plus cross-industry are represented and validated.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and this readiness evidence.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls restrict the lane to physician-compensation operations administration, including cycle grounding, claims and RVU evidence validation, attribution review, exception packaging, approval and payroll readiness, and closure evidence control without compensation-design, legal, coding, or executive drift.

Authority sources cover CMS physician claims processing, RVU references, CMS-1500 and 837P claim structure, E/M and incident-to attribution controls, Stark-law boundaries, NCCI coding controls, and HHS OIG physician-practice compliance expectations with refresh and decay metadata.

Scenario set covers missing industry evidence, evidence-free completion attempts, attribution conflicts, legal-boundary rejections, coding-compliance escalation, conflicting-policy handling, and tenant fact gaps.
Runtime remains specification-based with explicit promotion requirements for any later retained-implementation transition.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; compensation-model differences, academic-practice posture, and tenant-specific attribution rules are explicitly handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.
This package is ready for validated operations under specification-based execution controls.
